answer:
Drawing upon the profound wisdom of the Prophet, which serves as a clear light of guidance for the soul, we find that the verbal testimony of the *Shahada* must be rooted in the deepest depths of the heart [1, 2]. The Messenger of Allah taught that the individual who will be most fortunate to receive his intercession on the Day of Resurrection is the one who declares "None has the right to be worshipped but Allah" with absolute sincerity from the bottom of their heart [3]. This sincerity is the steadfast safeguard of the soul, for the Prophet assured Mu'adh that Allah has forbidden the Fire for anyone who testifies truthfully and sincerely to the Oneness of God and the messengership of Muhammad [1, 2].

To truly fulfill the personal dimension of the *Shahada*, a believer must be "assured of it in his heart," making this conviction a resilient foundation for his entire journey [4]. Beyond the verbal profession, the experiential dimension of faith is realized through a specific spiritual "sweetness" that transforms the inner self [5, 6]. According to the authentic traditions, this delight of faith is tasted when three qualities are present: Allah and His Messenger become dearer to the person than anything else, their love for others is directed solely for the sake of Allah, and they abhor returning to disbelief as much as they would dread being cast into a fire [5, 6].

This flavor of faith is further relished by the soul that is genuinely pleased and content with Allah as its Lord, Islam as its code of life, and Muhammad as its Messenger [7]. When such delight enters the heart and mixes with it completely, it creates an enduring framework of peace that cannot be disrupted [8]. Thus, the *Shahada* becomes far more than a nominal submission; it is a living reality where instruction is turned into action, allowing the believer to become a vibrant example of faith characterized by beauty and grace [9].

him ) said: There are three qualities for which anyone who is characterised by them will relish the sweetness of faith: he to whom Allah and His Messenger are dearer than all else; he who loves a man for Allah's sake alone; and he who has as great an abhorrence of returning to unbelief after Allah has rescued him from it as he has of being cast into Hell.
